message:
fix(controller): use 'ntm mail inbox SESSION' instead of broken --mail-project=SESSION in default prompt

Follow-up to c6e15d97 / 048d0b5d. Fresh-eyes review of the default
controller prompt caught a real bug in the mailbox-inspection line:

    ntm --robot-mail-check --mail-project={{.Session}}

After template substitution, this becomes
`--mail-project=myproject`. But `--mail-project` expects an Agent Mail
*project key* — which on this environment is the absolute project
directory path (e.g. `/data/projects/myproject`), not the NTM session
name. Running the literal command a controller would paste from this
prompt returns:

    list_agents failed: agentmail: resources/read failed:
    resource not found: Project not found

so the controller would conclude no mail is pending and stop checking
the inbox, exactly the opposite of the intended behavior.

Switch the line to `ntm mail inbox {{.Session}} --json`. The
`ntm mail inbox` subcommand does accept the NTM session name as a
positional argument and internally resolves it to the underlying
project key, so the substituted command now works against any NTM
session a controller is attached to. `--json` keeps the output
machine-readable for the controller loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -60,7 +60,7 @@ State inspection (read-only, safe to call in a loop; pass SESSION as the flag va
 - ntm --robot-activity={{.Session}}                        - Per-agent activity states (idle/busy/error)
 - ntm --robot-tail={{.Session}} --panes=N --lines=50       - Capture recent output from pane N
 - ntm --robot-attention --attention-session={{.Session}}   - Block until an agent needs attention (monitor loop)
-- ntm --robot-mail-check --mail-project={{.Session}}       - Check Agent Mail inbox for pending messages
+- ntm mail inbox {{.Session}} --json                       - Check Agent Mail inbox for pending messages
 
 Actions (mutating; use deliberately):
 - ntm send {{.Session}} --pane N "message"                 - Send message to a single pane (the message is a positional argument)
